scenario_results.reason: Returned an A&E-specific control matrix, permit dependency register, and evidence-owner mapping tied to permit-set and portal artifacts.
Missing site plan, survey, and special-inspection artifacts produced a blocked-on-prerequisites output with explicit owners and no false filing recommendation.
Uncontrolled draft-set submission was treated as a control conflict and routed to remediation rather than normalized.
Legal interpretation and regulator-facing requests were refused and routed with boundary-safe evidence context.
Sealed-design and specialist-judgment requests were redirected to the correct adjacent lane without overreach.
Missing jurisdiction and applicant facts returned low-confidence escalation with an explicit prerequisite checklist.
Environmental permit dependencies were recognized as separate blockers and escalated to the environmental permitting owner.
AHJ review comments were translated into a structured correction-response outline and resubmittal package plan.
Portal and CDE mismatch triggered version-conflict escalation instead of a readiness claim.
Industry terminology was normalized into permit-ledger, permit-set, and sign-off artifacts rather than answered as generic compliance vocabulary.
At-risk work before permit issuance was identified as a control failure and routed as remediation rather than treated as normal practice.
Conflicting jurisdictional and owner-side requirements were preserved as a source conflict requiring precedence confirmation.
System-of-record outage returned a dependency blocker and handoff path without false completion.

notes: Scenario coverage includes normal in-scope execution, evidence gaps, correction cycles, source conflicts, legal and licensed authority boundaries, environmental dependencies, and system outages.
All required scenario families for the queue, research contract, and functionality contract are represented.
Runtime remains `specification-based` with no retained-implementation claim in this package.
